brand.name: American Barber Institute
brand.tagline: Your Future. Your Career. Start Today.
brand.subtitle: New York's only dedicated barber school — changing lives for over 30 years.
brand.facility: 3,000 sq ft two-floor Midtown Manhattan facility
brand.license_authority: New York State Department of Education (BPSS)

programs.name: 500-Hour Master Barber Program
programs.summary: The complete, state-approved path to your New York Master Barber License. Cutting, fading, beard work, straight-razor shaving, sanitation, sterilization, barber history, NY State law, shop management, and full Board Exam prep — written and practical.
programs.details: Hands-on training in the live student clinic from week one. Choose Morning (Mon–Fri 8AM–2PM), Afternoon (Mon–Fri 2PM–8PM), or Weekend (Sat–Sun 9AM–7PM).

programs.name: 50-Hour Barber Refresher
programs.summary: A fast, focused refresher for licensed or returning barbers who want to sharpen speed, technique, and confidence on modern fades, beard work, and clipper-over-comb.
programs.details: Perfect after a career gap, a move to NY, or a license transfer.

programs.name: 3-Hour Contagious Diseases
programs.summary: NY State-required infection-control certification — sanitation, sterilization, blood-borne pathogens, OSHA, and compliance practices for the barbershop.
programs.details: A single session you can complete in an afternoon. Required for all NY barbers.

programs.name: 540-Hour Master Barber — Bronx
programs.summary: The full Master Barber certification program at the Westchester Square campus. Same curriculum and exam prep as the Manhattan program, in the Bronx.
programs.details: Same three flexible schedules. Same instructor-led clinic. Same NY State Master Barber License at the end.

programs.name: 3-Hour Contagious Diseases — Bronx
programs.summary: NY State-required infection-control certification — Bronx campus.

enrollment_requirements: High School Diploma, GED, or pass the ATB exam at ABI
Valid photo ID or driver's license

enrollment_steps.title: Apply
enrollment_steps.desc: Submit your information online or call admissions. The whole form takes less than two minutes.

enrollment_steps.title: Speak with an advisor
enrollment_steps.desc: An admissions advisor walks you through tuition options, the GI Bill / ACCES-VR process, and the schedule that fits your life.

enrollment_steps.title: Start training
enrollment_steps.desc: New cohorts start the first Monday of every month. Complete enrollment, gather your tools list, and step onto the clinic floor.

why_choose: New York's only dedicated barber school — barbering is all we do.
Licensed by the NY State Department of Education (BPSS).
30+ years of proven results with 10,000+ graduates.
3,000 sq ft two-floor facility in Midtown Manhattan.
Veterans & GI Bill® benefits accepted: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Ch. 33), VR&E (Ch. 31), Montgomery GI Bill, DEA (Ch. 35).
Hands-on training in a real barber clinic from day one.
Real barbershop skills taught within the first few weeks.
Full-time Job Placement Office on graduation.
Two campuses: Manhattan and the Bronx.

career_earnings.desc: Building a client base, refining technique, learning the rhythm of a working shop floor.
Loyal clientele, faster service, increased income, brand starting to travel.
Full control of schedule, pricing, and brand. Most graduates aiming for ownership land here within five years.

earnings_note: Earnings figures are estimates only and are not guaranteed. Actual income will vary based on individual effort, hours worked, location, and market conditions.

instructors.name: David "King David" Ayeoribe
instructors.bio: Over 50 years of barbering experience and more than 30 years of teaching. The longest-serving barber instructor in New York City and one of the most respected names in NY barbering education. King David has personally trained generations of master barbers — from first-timers to shop owners across the five boroughs.

instructors.name: Harold "Barkim" Brown
instructors.bio: Decades of professional barbering and a relentless eye for detail. Barkim runs the floor like a working shop — fast, exact, and patient with every student who steps to a chair.

instructors.name: Barry Brown
instructors.bio: Brings deep experience from the NY barber scene to a curriculum focused on modern fades, beard work, and the discipline of running a real chair under pressure.

instructors.name: Richard
instructors.bio: English and Spanish instruction. Richard's brother Mike owns Diamond Fadez Barbershop just one block from our Manhattan campus — a two-generation family story rooted in ABI.

testimonials.name: Jerrick Matthews
testimonials.quote: The level of knowledge and training is superb. One of the best teachers around — King David — will show you everything.

testimonials.name: Carlos Perez
testimonials.quote: I have King David as my instructor and he has been awesome. He has 30 years of experience and gives us great techniques.

testimonials.name: Zyee Fin
testimonials.quote: I'm currently enrolled and I'm happy with the progress from learning from the teachers and classmates. Nothing but positivity.

testimonials.name: Richard Cacace
testimonials.quote: The best barber school in New York — way better than the Arrojo Barber Academy. Real barber culture.

testimonials.name: Vincybie Lee
testimonials.quote: Only $3 for a regular haircut and it's not bad at all. Great that we could help student trainees.

testimonials.name: Tina Banee
testimonials.quote: Donovan gave me a great haircut. He did exactly what I asked. Well worth $3 plus a $7 tip.

partners.name: Levels Barbershop
partners.desc: Founded 1996 on 125th Street in Harlem. Multi-location empire including The Antique Barber product line and Barber World TV. Several Nuru-family co-owners are ABI graduates; many licensed barbers on staff are alumni.
partners.why: Proof that ABI graduates can build iconic businesses and mentor the next generation.

partners.name: Diamond Fadez
partners.desc: Combined barbershop and tattoo studio one block from ABI's Manhattan campus. Owner Mike is an ABI graduate; his brother Richard teaches at ABI as a bilingual instructor.
partners.why: Two generations of one family — taught and now teaching at the same school.

partners.name: Untouchable Cutz
partners.desc: Respected modern barbershop with a team carrying 50+ combined years of experience. Owner Jimmy opened shop within two years of earning his ABI license. Actively hires ABI graduates and offers private tutoring.
partners.why: Rapid entrepreneurial success — and a commitment to passing the craft forward.

partners.name: Expo Gentlemen Salon
partners.locations: Staten Island (326 New Dorp Lane) · Brooklyn
partners.desc: Multi-location established salon. Owner Joe consistently hires ABI graduates to staff both locations.
partners.why: Trust built shop-by-shop through consistent graduate performance.

partners.name: Otis & Finn
partners.locations: Long Island City · Court Square · Greenpoint · Williamsburg
partners.desc: State-of-the-art barbershop featured in GQ, BuzzFeed, HuffPost, FOX5, and PIX11. ABI graduates anchor every team; graduate James Cavanaugh is featured in the official Otis & Finn bio.
partners.why: Public partnerships where employers publicly recognize and invest in alumni talent.

partners.name: NYC Barber Shop Museum
partners.locations: Columbus Ave & E 70th St, Manhattan · Sunny Isles, Miami
partners.desc: The first museum of its kind — a working barbershop housing century-old artifacts. Operated by fourth-generation Master Barber Arthur Rubinoff. 501(c)(3) non-profit.
partners.why: "To work at the NYC Barber Shop Museum is to step into a lineage." Four-generation professional standard.

haircut_clinic.intro: Our student barber clinic is open to the public Monday through Friday. You'll sit in the chair of a future master barber — every cut supervised by a NY-licensed instructor on the floor.
haircut_clinic.what_to_expect: Call ahead or walk in based on availability. Consult with your student barber on the style you want. All work is supervised by licensed instructors — student barbers are training, not licensed professionals, and you'll know exactly who's in your chair.

job_placement.intro: Every ABI graduate meets with our Job Placement Office. We don't just hand you a license — we put you in the seat of your first chair.
services: Direct referrals to our partner shops (Levels, Diamond Fadez, Otis & Finn, Untouchable Cutz, Expo Gentlemen, NYC Barber Shop Museum)
Independent shop-owner mentorship for grads who want to open their own
job_placement.outcomes: Many of our partner shops are owned by alumni. Many of their licensed staff are alumni. Our placement office runs the pipeline you'll step into the day you pass the State Board.

veterans.title: Veterans & GI Bill® Benefits
veterans.intro: ABI is approved to certify enrollment for veteran education benefits. We accept:
items: Montgomery GI Bill® (Active Duty & Selected Reserve)
veterans.outro: Coverage may pay all or part of your tuition. Talk to admissions about certifying your benefits before your start date.

accesvr.title: ACCES-VR
accesvr.intro: ACCES-VR (Adult Career and Continuing Education Services — Vocational Rehabilitation) is a New York State program that supports individuals with documented disabilities in obtaining and maintaining employment.
items: Coverage may include tuition, tools, and supplies
Contact your local ACCES-VR office to begin
ABI assists with the referral and certification process

licensing.title: NY State Board of Barbering
items: 500-hour Master Barber program covers both written and practical exam prep
Master Barber License required to practice professionally in New York

tools.title: Tools & Supplies
items: Students purchase their own tools, books, and supplies
ABI provides a detailed tools list at registration
Buy from ABI directly or from approved third-party suppliers

faqs.q: How long does the Master Barber program take?
faqs.a: The 500-Hour Master Barber program can be completed in as little as 4 months full-time (Morning or Afternoon, Mon–Fri). The Weekend track typically completes in 6–7 months. The 50-Hour Refresher is a 2-week intensive. The 3-Hour Contagious Diseases course is a single afternoon.

faqs.q: Do I need prior experience to enroll?
faqs.a: No prior barbering experience is required. We train you from the fundamentals — sanitation, posture, blade angle — and progress to advanced fading, beard work, straight-razor shaving, color, and chair-side business skills.
New classes begin the first Monday of every month. The next cohort starts Monday, July 6, 2026. Apply, gather your documents, and be ready for the next start date — there's almost never a long wait.

A Social Security card, a High School Diploma / GED (or pass the ATB exam at ABI), proof of address, a valid photo ID, being at least 17, and a $500 down payment (which includes a $100 registration fee).

faqs.q: How much does the program cost?
faqs.a: The 500-Hour Master Barber program is $4,600 on the Afternoon and Weekend tracks; the Morning track is $5,600. The 50-Hour Refresher is $1,500. The 3-Hour Contagious Diseases course is call-for-pricing. All programs include flexible weekly payment plans after the down payment.

Yes. Every program includes a weekly payment plan after your down payment. Examples: Morning is $500 down + 17 weekly payments of $300. Afternoon is $500 down + 16 weekly payments of $250 + $100 final. Weekend is $550 down + 27 weekly payments of $150.
On completing the 500-Hour Master Barber program you'll be fully prepared for the New York State Board of Barbering exam — both written and practical — and your Master Barber License.

Yes. On graduation you'll meet with our Job Placement Office for 1-on-1 career counseling, resume and portfolio review, mock interviews, and direct referrals to our partner shops including Levels, Diamond Fadez, Otis & Finn, Untouchable Cutz, Expo Gentlemen, and the NYC Barber Shop Museum.

faqs.q: Do you accept GI Bill® or VA benefits?
faqs.a: Yes. ABI accepts the Post-9/11 GI Bill® (Ch. 33), VR&E (Ch. 31), Montgomery GI Bill®, and DEA (Ch. 35) education benefits. We certify your enrollment with the VA on your behalf.

ACCES-VR is the NY State Adult Career and Continuing Education Services — Vocational Rehabilitation program. It supports individuals with documented disabilities and may cover tuition, tools, and supplies. Talk to your local ACCES-VR office, and we'll handle the school-side paperwork.

faqs.q: Is ABI licensed by the state?
faqs.a: Yes. American Barber Institute is licensed by the New York State Department of Education through the Bureau of Proprietary School Supervision (BPSS), and we've served New York for over 30 years.

faqs.q: Can I get a haircut at ABI?
faqs.a: Yes — the public is welcome for affordable student haircuts (typically $3). It's a great way to support trainees while getting a clean, supervised cut. Booking: (856) 316-1551.

faqs.q: Do I need to buy my own tools?
faqs.a: Yes. Students are responsible for purchasing their own barbering tools, books, and supplies. ABI provides a detailed tools list at registration, with the option to buy directly from us or from approved third-party suppliers.
Yes. Spanish-speaking admissions advisors are available. Call (212) 290-0278 to speak with Spanish admissions at the Manhattan campus.

We keep instructor-to-student ratios low so every student gets real hands-on time on the floor. You're not learning by lecture — you're learning by chair time, in a working clinic, with an instructor inches away when you need them.
